LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JEFFREY CHU'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE HIS FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WITHIN 10 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS ORDER. PLAINTIFF DID NOT UNREASONABLY DELAY IN SEEKING LEAVE TO AMEND AND DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT ESTABLISHED THEY WILL BE PREJUDICED BY THE AMENDMENT. TRIAL IS MORE THAN TWO MONTHS OUT AND DISCOVERY IS STILL ONGOING. DEFENDANTS' REQUEST FOR A TRIAL CONTINUANCE IS DENIED AT THIS TIME. IF A TRIAL CONTINUANCE IS NECESSARY, THE PARTIES MAY REQUEST ONE IN THE PRESIDING JUDGE'S DEPARTMENT. (SEE SF LOCAL RULE 6.0.). JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; DEPT. 302. (302/EHG)

DISCOVERY, 302, AS TO PLAINTIFF JEFFREY CHU'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT LEAP TRANSITS FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET ONE, AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS, SO MUCH OF THE MOTION THAT SEEKS SANCTIONS IS DENIED WITH PREJUDICE; AND THE MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES IS DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE; AND BY ON OR BEFORE JULY 27, 2015 PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT LEAP ARE ORDERED TO ENGAGE IN A FURTHER MEET AND CONFER EITHER BY TELEPHONE, OR PREFERABLY IN PERSON, TO CONTINUE THEIR DELIBERATIONS UNDERTAKEN IN MAY, 2015 TOWARDS EFFECTING DEFENDANT LEAP'S RESPONSE TO THE FIRST REQUEST IN FORM AND SUBSTANCE ACCEPTABLE TO BOTH PARTIES, USING BOTH AS A STARTING POINT AND GUIDE THE MATERIAL SET FORTH IN THE FIRST TWO PARAGRAPHS OF SECTION 3 OF DEFENDANT LEAP'S MEMORANDUM IN OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION DATED JULY 2, 2015, WHICH PURPORTS TO SET FORTH A SUMMARY OF THE CONSENSUS REACHED TO DATE ON THE OUTSTANDING ISSUES UNDER THE THE RESPONSE TO THE FIRST REQUEST AND DEFENDANT LEAP'S CONCURRENCE IN IT; AND BY ON OR BEFORE JULY 31, 2015 THE PARTIES SHALL HAVE REDUCED THEIR AGREEMENT ON DOCUMENT PRODUCTION UNDER THE FIRST REQUEST TO WRITING; AND BY ON OR BEFORE AUGUST 15, 2015, DEFENDANT LEAP SHALL HAVE PRODUCED TO PLAINTIFF ALL OF THE DOCUMENTS WHICH THE PARTIES SHALL HAVE AGREED ON AS AFORESAID. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE PRO TEM: ADRIAN COLLEY, CLERK: SEAN KANE, NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF JEFFREY CHU's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint is granted. Plaintiff may file his First Amended Complaint within 10 days of entry of this order. Plaintiff did not unreasonably delay in seeking leave to amend and Defendants have not established they will be prejudiced by the amendment. Trial is more than two months out and discovery is still ongoing. Defendants' request for a trial continuance is denied at this time. If a trial continuance is necessary, the parties may request one in the Presiding Judge's department. (See SF Local Rule 6.0.). Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

Plaintiff Jeffrey Chu'S Motion To Compel Defendant Leap Transits Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Jul-16-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Plaintiff Jeffrey Chu's Motion To Compel Defendant Leap Transits Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions is taken off calendar as a duplicate entry. (302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Leap Transits Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jul-16-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to PLAINTIFF JEFFREY CHU'S Motion To Compel Defendant Leap Transits Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One, And For Monetary Sanctions, so much of the motion that seeks sanctions is denied with prejudice; and the motion to compel further responses is denied, without prejudice; and by on or before July 27, 2015 Plaintiff and defendant Leap are ordered to engage in a further meet and confer either by telephone, or preferably in person, to continue their deliberations undertaken in May, 2015 towards effecting defendant Leap's response to the First Request in form and substance acceptable to both parties, using both as a starting point and guide the material set forth in the first two paragraphs of section 3 of defendant Leap's memorandum in opposition to the motion dated July 2, 2015, which purports to set forth a summary of the consensus reached to date on the outstanding issues under the the response to the First Request and defendant Leap's concurrence in it; and by on or before July 31, 2015 the parties shall have reduced their agreement on document production under the First Request to writing; and by on or before August 15, 2015, defendant Leap shall have produced to plaintiff all of the documents which the parties shall have agreed on as aforesaid. The prevailing party shall prepare and submit a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge Pro Tem: Adrian Colley, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify And Order For The Appointment Of Independent Counsel For Corporate Defendant
Off Calendar Apr-27-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Larry B. Panek, of Carroll, Burdick & McDonough LLP, Tel. 415.743.2206, for plaintiffs;
Wendy J. Brenner, of Cooley LLP, Tel. 650.843.5371, for defendants;
No other appearances.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF JEFFREY CHU'S Motion To Disqualify And Order For The Appointment Of Independent Counsel For Corporate Defendant is Denied. Counsel for defendant provided the Court and opposing counsel a copy of letter regarding full disclosure or waiver of a potential or actual conflict in compliance with CRPC 3-310(C) and 3-600(E). None of plaintiffs' allegations necessarily result in actual conflict between the individual defendants and the corporate defendant and, in all events, any conflict that does exist or could arise may be waived per CRPC 3-310(C) and 3-600(E). Order signed. Judge Harold Kahn; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Denise Doucette, CSR No. 5963; Dept. 302.
